method_atom: an information-theoretic analysis of a nonlinear signal-transmission channel in which the intractable nonlinearity is recast as a linear channel corrupted by multiplicative (signal-dependent) noise, yielding an analytically tractable capacity; unlike the additive-noise linear case, the channel capacity does not increase without bound as signal power grows but saturates at a finite maximum.
object_atom: the transmission of information as light propagating through an optical fiber, where Kerr-type nonlinearities of the medium limit the achievable communication rate.
brief_reason: The multiplicative-noise recasting of a nonlinear channel and its saturating-capacity result carry across (the abstract itself notes applicability to neurobiological sensory transduction), while light propagating in an optical fiber is the domain-specific object.
